title: Hussein-McMahon correspondence
summary: British wartime correspondence encouraged Arab revolt expectations while leaving later sovereignty questions ambiguous.
data: Ambiguity becomes a recurring grievance in Arab nationalist narratives.

title: Sykes-Picot Agreement
summary: British and French spheres of influence were sketched for post-Ottoman Arab provinces.
data: Boundary-making becomes a KG edge: colonial agreement -> mandates -> state borders.

title: Balfour Declaration
summary: Britain supported a national home for the Jewish people in Palestine while mentioning civil and religious rights of existing non-Jewish communities.
data: Core contested claims: national home vs existing population rights.

title: San Remo conference and Mandate system
summary: Former Ottoman provinces were reorganized under British and French mandates.
data: Creates modern governance units later central to Syria, Lebanon, Iraq, Palestine and Transjordan.

title: British Mandate for Palestine confirmed
summary: Mandate text incorporated the Balfour framework and British administrative control.
data: Institutional root of later partition debates.

title: Arab Revolt in Palestine
actors: Palestinian Arab groups; British authorities; Jewish communities
summary: A major revolt against British rule and Jewish immigration was suppressed.
data: Shows early mass mobilization, policing, insurgency, land and migration disputes.

title: UN General Assembly Resolution 181 recommends partition
actors: UNGA; Jewish Agency; Arab Higher Committee
summary: The UN General Assembly recommended independent Arab and Jewish states and a special international regime for Jerusalem.
data: Vote: 33 for, 13 against, 10 abstentions; recommended two states plus Jerusalem internationalization.

title: Israel declares independence; 1948 Arab-Israeli War begins
actors: Israel; Egypt; Jordan; Syria; Lebanon; Iraq; Palestinian Arabs
summary: Israel declared independence as the British Mandate ended; neighboring Arab states intervened; the war reshaped borders beyond the 1947 plan.
data: U.S. State archive notes Israel gained some territory allocated to Palestinian Arabs; Egypt retained Gaza and Jordan retained West Bank/East Jerusalem.

title: Palestinian refugee crisis and UNRWA context
actors: Palestinian refugees; Israel; Arab states; UN
summary: Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians became refugees during/after the 1948 war, creating a persistent refugee and right-of-return issue.
data: Often cited around 700,000+ displaced; exact figures and causation remain contested.

title: Armistice lines / Green Line
summary: Armistice agreements produced lines that became reference points for later diplomacy and occupation debates.
data: Creates important geospatial entity: 1949 Armistice Line / Green Line.

title: Suez Crisis
actors: Egypt; Israel; Britain; France; US; USSR; UN
summary: Egypt nationalized the Suez Canal; Israel, Britain and France attacked; U.S.-Soviet pressure led to withdrawal.
data: Shows global powers constraining regional war.

title: PLO founded
summary: The Palestine Liberation Organization became a central umbrella for Palestinian national politics and armed struggle.
data: Entity type: political organization / national movement.

title: Six-Day War
theater: Sinai; Gaza; West Bank; East Jerusalem; Golan Heights
summary: Israel captured Sinai, Gaza, West Bank, East Jerusalem and the Golan Heights.
data: Creates occupation nodes and land-for-peace framework; UN Security Council Resolution 242 follows.

title: Black September in Jordan
summary: Jordanian state forces clashed with Palestinian armed organizations; PLO leadership later relocated to Lebanon.
data: Links Palestinian armed politics to Lebanon civil-war dynamics.

title: Yom Kippur / October War
summary: Egypt and Syria attacked Israeli positions; the war triggered superpower crisis diplomacy and oil embargo politics.
data: Produces later disengagement agreements and Camp David pathway.

title: Lebanese Civil War
actors: Maronite parties; Palestinian groups; Lebanese left; Syria; Israel; militias
summary: Lebanon’s sectarian-political order collapsed into multi-sided civil war with Palestinian, Syrian and Israeli interventions.
data: Multi-actor graph with militias, foreign armies, confessional parties.

title: Camp David Accords
summary: Egypt and Israel agreed to a framework leading to the 1979 treaty.
data: First Arab state peace treaty with Israel; Sinai returned to Egypt.

title: Iranian Revolution
actors: Iranian revolutionaries; Pahlavi state; US; regional monarchies
summary: Iran’s monarchy fell; the Islamic Republic became a central revisionist actor in Gulf and Levant politics.
data: Entity shift: Iran/Pahlavi -> Islamic Republic; alters patronage networks.

title: Egypt-Israel Peace Treaty
summary: Egypt recognized Israel and Sinai withdrawal was scheduled.
data: Egypt suspended by Arab League for a period; peace removes largest Arab military from Arab-Israeli war coalition.

title: Iran-Iraq War
actors: Iran; Iraq; Gulf monarchies; US; USSR
summary: Iraq invaded Iran; an eight-year war killed hundreds of thousands and militarized Gulf security.
data: Graph layer: state war + oil shipping + chemical weapons + external backing.

title: Israel invades Lebanon; PLO expelled from Beirut
actors: Israel; PLO; Syria; Lebanese militias; US mediators
summary: Israel invaded Lebanon after cross-border attacks and assassination attempt context; PLO leadership evacuated from Beirut.
data: Hezbollah emerges later amid occupation and Iranian support.

title: First Intifada begins
summary: A mass Palestinian uprising against Israeli occupation began in the occupied Palestinian territory.
data: UN describes mass uprising in 1987; Hamas founded during this period.

title: Palestine National Council proclaims State of Palestine
summary: The PNC proclaimed the State of Palestine and later diplomacy emphasized two-state recognition.
data: Adds entity: State of Palestine / diplomatic recognition network.

title: Iraq invades Kuwait
actors: Iraq; Kuwait; US-led coalition; UN Security Council
summary: Iraq annexed Kuwait, triggering UN-backed coalition war.
data: Links Gulf security architecture to permanent U.S. regional military presence.

title: Madrid Conference
actors: US; USSR; Israel; Arab states; Palestinians
summary: Post-Gulf War diplomacy opened multilateral and bilateral Arab-Israeli tracks.
data: Precursor to Oslo and Jordan-Israel treaty.

title: Oslo I Accords
summary: Israel and PLO mutually recognized each other; the Palestinian Authority framework was created.
data: Creates PA governance node; final-status issues deferred.

title: Jordan-Israel Peace Treaty
summary: Jordan became the second Arab state to sign a peace treaty with Israel.
data: Diplomatic edge: Jordan -> recognizes -> Israel.

title: Second Intifada begins
summary: A second uprising followed failed final-status talks and rising tensions.
data: High-casualty urban conflict, suicide bombings, incursions, separation barrier debate.

title: Arab Peace Initiative
actors: Arab League; Saudi Arabia; Israel; Palestinians
summary: Arab states offered normalization with Israel in exchange for withdrawal from occupied territories and a Palestinian state.
data: Important unrealized diplomatic template.

title: U.S.-led invasion of Iraq
actors: US; UK; Iraq; coalition; Iraqi factions
summary: The invasion toppled Saddam Hussein and opened a long insurgency and sectarian conflict.
data: Regional effect: Iran influence in Iraq grows; jihadist networks evolve.

title: Israel disengages from Gaza
summary: Israel removed settlements and permanent ground forces from Gaza but retained control over airspace, maritime access and crossings with Egypt/Israel disputes.
data: Legal status of occupation remains contested.

title: Israel-Hezbollah War
actors: Israel; Hezbollah; Lebanon; Iran; Syria; UNIFIL
summary: Hezbollah captured Israeli soldiers; Israel launched large-scale operations; UN Security Council Resolution 1701 expanded UNIFIL role.
data: Shows deterrence between Israel and Hezbollah after 2006.

title: Hamas wins Palestinian legislative elections
summary: Hamas won legislative elections, deepening international and intra-Palestinian tensions.
data: Leads to 2007 Gaza-Fatah split.

title: Hamas takes control of Gaza; blockade era intensifies
summary: Hamas seized Gaza from Fatah; Israel and Egypt tightened controls over Gaza crossings.
data: Creates separate governance nodes: PA/Ramallah and Hamas/Gaza.

title: Gaza War / Operation Cast Lead
summary: Large-scale Israel-Hamas conflict after rocket fire and blockade-related escalation.
data: Template for later Gaza wars: rockets, airstrikes, humanitarian crisis, ceasefire mediation.

title: Syrian uprising becomes civil war
actors: Syrian government; opposition; Kurds; jihadists; Iran; Hezbollah; Russia; Turkey; US
summary: Protests against the Assad government escalated into a multi-sided civil war and internationalized proxy conflict.
data: Displacement over 13 million by later UNHCR reporting; conflict intersects with ISIS and Kurdish autonomy.

title: Yemen uprising and transition crisis
actors: Saleh government; protesters; Houthis; Southern movement; Gulf Cooperation Council
summary: Arab Spring protests led to a GCC-mediated transition but unresolved power struggles persisted.
data: Precursor to 2014 Houthi takeover and Saudi-led intervention.

title: ISIS declares caliphate
actors: ISIS; Iraq; Syria; Kurds; US-led coalition; Iran-backed militias
summary: ISIS captured territory across Iraq and Syria and declared a caliphate.
data: Adds transnational non-state actor controlling territory.

title: Gaza War / Operation Protective Edge
summary: Another major Gaza war followed kidnapping/murder crisis, rocket fire and Israeli operations.
data: Demonstrates recurrence of escalation cycles.

title: Houthis take Sanaa
summary: Houthi movement captured Yemen’s capital, Sanaa.
data: Houthi control creates later Red Sea and Iran-aligned axis links.

title: Saudi-led intervention in Yemen
actors: Saudi Arabia; UAE; Houthis; Hadi government; Iran
summary: Saudi-led coalition intervened against the Houthis after Hadi fled.
data: Yemen becomes humanitarian crisis and regional proxy war.

title: JCPOA nuclear agreement
summary: Iran and world powers agreed to limits on Iran’s nuclear program in exchange for sanctions relief.
data: Nuclear file is a major conflict-driver edge: sanctions -> enrichment -> strikes risk.

title: US withdraws from JCPOA
summary: The United States withdrew from the nuclear deal and reimposed sanctions.
data: Escalates Iran-US tensions; affects Gulf shipping and proxy conflict.

title: Attacks on Saudi oil facilities
summary: Abqaiq and Khurais oil facilities were attacked; US and Saudi officials blamed Iran, which denied involvement.
data: Critical infrastructure node: oil facilities, drones/missiles, attribution dispute.

title: US kills Qassem Soleimani
actors: US; IRGC-Quds Force; Iraq; Iran-backed militias
summary: A U.S. drone strike killed IRGC-Quds Force commander Qassem Soleimani in Baghdad.
data: Regional network shock: IRGC -> proxy network -> US basing in Iraq.

title: Abraham Accords
theater: UAE; Bahrain; Israel; Morocco; Sudan; US
actors: Israel; UAE; Bahrain; Morocco; Sudan; US
summary: Several Arab states normalized relations with Israel under U.S.-backed agreements.
data: Creates normalization track separate from Palestinian final-status settlement.

title: Gaza-Israel conflict and Jerusalem unrest
actors: Israel; Hamas; Palestinian civilians; Israeli civilians
summary: Tensions around Jerusalem and evictions contributed to Hamas rocket fire and Israeli airstrikes.
data: Urban mixed-city violence and Gaza conflict interconnected.

title: Yemen truce begins
summary: A UN-mediated truce reduced cross-border attacks and major front-line fighting, though political settlement remained elusive.
data: Fragile de-escalation node; maritime attacks resumed later.

title: Hamas-led attack on southern Israel
actors: Hamas; Palestinian Islamic Jihad; Israel; civilians
summary: Hamas-led forces attacked southern Israel, killing civilians and soldiers and taking hostages; Israel launched a large-scale war in Gaza.
data: Core trigger of 2023-2026 Gaza and regional escalation.

title: Israel-Hamas war in Gaza
actors: Israel; Hamas; PIJ; civilians; Egypt; Qatar; US
summary: Israel’s military campaign and Hamas governance/armed resistance produced massive destruction, displacement and recurring ceasefire-hostage negotiations.
data: OCHA 2026 reports most Gaza residents displaced, severe aid access restrictions, water and disease risks.

title: Houthi Red Sea attacks linked to Gaza war
actors: Houthis; Israel-linked shipping; US; UK; commercial shipping
summary: Houthis began attacking ships they linked to Israel or Gaza conflict, disrupting Red Sea trade routes.
data: HRW describes Red Sea attacks as possible war crimes; Reuters 2026 reports renewed threats to Israeli navigation.

title: Iran-Israel direct exchange after embassy-consulate strike cycle
summary: Iran and Israel moved from shadow conflict to direct attacks after strike/retaliation sequences.
data: Escalation ladder: proxy -> direct drone/missile exchange.

title: Lebanon conflict intensifies after nearly a year of border fire
summary: After months of exchanges following Oct. 2023, Israeli airstrikes intensified and Lebanon displacement surged.
data: Reuters/UN reported around 1.2 million displaced in 2024 phase.

title: Ceasefire agreement referenced in Gaza reporting
summary: OCHA and Reuters 2026 reports refer to a ceasefire announced on 10 October 2025 but continuing violations and unresolved phases.
data: OCHA: 936 fatalities and 2,903 injuries in Gaza from ceasefire announcement to 3 Jun 2026 per MoH.

title: UN warns Yemen crisis will worsen in 2026
summary: UN warned Yemen’s humanitarian situation would worsen amid funding cuts and access constraints.
data: Estimated 21 million in need of aid in 2026; up from 19.5 million; 2025 funding about 28% of target.

title: Lebanon drawn into wider regional war
actors: Hezbollah; Israel; Iran; US; Lebanese civilians
summary: Reuters reports Lebanon was drawn into wider war in early March 2026 after Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel in solidarity with Iran under U.S.-Israeli attack.
data: By 5 Jun 2026, Lebanon aid appeal doubled to $639.9m; more than 3,500 killed in Israeli strikes per Lebanese authorities.

title: U.S.-brokered Lebanon ceasefire begins but fighting continues
summary: A U.S.-brokered Lebanon ceasefire came into effect after April 16 announcement; fighting persisted in the south.
data: Lebanese defense minister: 3,491 Israeli airstrikes, 407 controlled demolitions and six razing operations from Apr 17-Jun 7.

title: OCHA Gaza situation report snapshot
actors: OCHA; MoH Gaza; Israel; humanitarian partners
summary: OCHA reported daily strikes, constraints on aid, water shortages, pest/rodent risks, and West Bank settler violence/demolition patterns.
data: May aid pallets: 51,900 at Kerem Shalom/Zikim; prices +235% vs pre-Oct 2023; West Bank >950 settler incidents in 2026.

title: Reuters reports Gaza strikes and control-zone expansion
actors: Israel; Hamas; Egypt mediators; Palestinian civilians
summary: Reuters reported six Palestinians killed in strikes as Cairo ceasefire talks continued; Israel controlled more than 60% of Gaza and Netanyahu ordered expansion toward 70%.
data: Nearly entire 2 million population living in small coastal strip; over 950 killed since truce according to health officials.

title: Houthis threaten Israeli shipping in Red Sea
actors: Houthis; Israel-linked shipping; Iran; commercial shipping
summary: Houthis announced a ban on Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, heightening concerns over misidentification and rerouting.
data: Red Sea remained essential because Strait of Hormuz closure since February 2026 increased reliance on alternate oil route, per Reuters.
